subject: Physics
options: Both A and R are correct but R is NOT the correct explanation of A
A is not correct but R is correct
A is correct but R is not correct
Both A and R are correct and R is the correct explanation of A
solution:
When light travels through a medium, its velocity depends on the density of the medium.

The formula to calculate the speed of light can be written as

v=νλ   ...(1)

From equation (1), it can be concluded that higher the speed, higher is the wavelength. Hence, the wavelength of light depends on the refractive index of the medium.

Because of changed wavelength the phase difference changes while the two waves travel the same distance.

Thus, both A and R are correct and R is the correct explanation of A.

subject: Physics
solution:
Let's consider the following diagram-

The relation between the object distance, the image distance and the radius of curvature of the spherical surface can be written as

μ2v-μ1u=μ2-μ1R   ...1

Substitute the values of the known parameters into equation (1) and solve to calculate the required image distance.

1.5v-1-15=1.5-1.030⇒1.5v=160-115=-120⇒v=-30

Hence, the image is formed at a distance of 30 cm from the pole on the same direction as the object is placed.

subject: Physics
solution:
Let the fish be at a depth x from the surface and let the bird be at height y from the surface. So, with respect to fish

y'=μy+x⇒y'=4y3+x

Differentiating with respect to time,

dy'dt=4dy3dt+dxdt

⇒4y3+dxdt=-12

⇒43·dydt=-12+8=-4

⇒dydt=-3 m s-1

subject: Physics
solution:
For total internal reflection on face $A C$ $\theta>$ critical angle $(C)$ and $\sin \theta \geq \sin C$

$$
\begin{aligned}
& \sin \theta \geq \frac{1}{w_{\mu_g}} \\
& \sin \theta \geq \frac{\mu_w}{\mu_g} \Rightarrow \sin \theta \geq \frac{\frac{4}{3}}{\frac{3}{2}} \\
& \therefore \quad \sin \theta \geq \frac{8}{9} .
\end{aligned}
$$

options: <math><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mn>40</mn><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi></math> from the convergent lens, twice the size of the object

solution:
First refraction, u=-40 cm; f=+20 cm
From lens formula, 1v-1u=1f
1v-1-40=120⇒1v=120-140
v=+40 cm
m1=-1
First reflection, u=-20 cm;f=-10 cm
From mirror formula, 1v+1u=1f
1v-120=1-10
1v=1-10+120⇒v=-20 cm
m2=-1
Second refraction, u=-40 cm,  f=+20 cm
From lens formula, 1v-1-40=120⇒v=+40 cm
m3=-1
mtotal=m1×m2×m3=-1
Finally, image will be at distance 40 cm  from lens and is of same size as object.

subject: Physics
solution:
By using Snell's law, n1sinθ1=n2sinθ2, the refractive index is 

μ=sin45°sin30°=1212=2

Also using the relation n=cv where n is the refractive index, c is the velocity of light in air and v is the velocity in the medium

⇒cair v =2=λ1 λ2

The velocities are given by 

v=νλ2c=νλ1

⇒λ1=2λ2 and ν1=ν2.

The frequency of light does not change when the medium changes.

subject: Physics
solution:
Assuming that the right angled prism is an isoceles prism, so the other angles will be 45° each.

⇒ Each incident ray will make an angle of 45° with the normal at face PR.

⇒ The wavelength corresponding to which the incidence angle is less than the critical angle, will pass through PR.

⇒θC= critical angle

⇒θC=sin-11μ

⇒ If θC≥45°

the light ray will pass

⇒θCRed=sin-111.27=51.94°

Red will pass.

⇒θCGreen=sin-111.42=44.76°

Green will not pass

⇒θCBlue=sin-111.49=42.15°

Blue will not pass

⇒ So only red will pass through PR.

subject: Physics
solution:
Given,

Angle of prism, A=1°

Refractive index of prism, μ=1.5

For this prism, minimum angle of deviation is given by

δ=(μ-1)A

⇒δ=(1.5-1)×1°=12=510=N10

⇒N=5

subject: Physics
solution:
Using the lens maker's formula:

$\dfrac{1}{f} = (\mu - 1) \left( \dfrac{1}{R_1} - \dfrac{1}{R_2} \right)$

For an equiconvex lens, $R_1 = R$ and $R_2 = -R$. Given $\mu = 1.4$:

$\dfrac{1}{f} = (1.4 - 1) \left( \dfrac{1}{R} - \dfrac{1}{-R} \right)$

$\dfrac{1}{f} = 0.4 \times \dfrac{2}{R}$

$\dfrac{1}{f} = \dfrac{0.8}{R}$

$\dfrac{f}{R} = \dfrac{1}{0.8} = 1.25$

Answer: $1.25$

solution:
As we know,
$\frac{1}{\mathrm{f}}=\left[\frac{\mu_{\mathrm{L}}}{\mu_{\mathrm{m}}}-1\right]\left[\frac{1}{\mathrm{R}_1}-\frac{1}{\mathrm{R}_2}\right]$
For air $\mu_{\mathrm{m}}=1$
$\begin{aligned} & \frac{1}{12}=[1.6-1]\left[\frac{1}{\mathrm{R}_1}-\frac{1}{\mathrm{R}_2}\right] \\ & \frac{1}{12}=\frac{6}{10}\left[\frac{1}{\mathrm{R}_1}-\frac{1}{\mathrm{R}_2}\right] \\ & {\left[\frac{1}{\mathrm{R}_1}-\frac{1}{\mathrm{R}_2}\right]=\frac{10}{72}}\end{aligned}$
For water
$\frac{1}{\mathrm{f}}=\left[\frac{1.6}{1.28}-1\right]\left[\frac{10}{72}\right]=\frac{32}{128} \times \frac{10}{72}$
$\begin{aligned} & \frac{1}{\mathrm{f}}=\frac{1}{4} \times \frac{10}{72} \\ & \mathrm{f}=28.8 \mathrm{~cm} \\ & \mathrm{f}=288 \mathrm{~mm}\end{aligned}$

subject: Physics
solution:
Speed of light in medium is related to speed of light in vacuum c and refractive index μ by v=cμ.

Then, we have μ=cv=c0.2c

⇒μ=5

Refractive index in terms of relative permeability μrand relative permittivityεr  of the medium is stated as μ=ϵr μr

⇒ϵr=μ2μr

∴ ϵrμ=μμr=5

Hence, the value of x=5.

subject: Physics
options: Resolving power will be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>4</mn></mfrac></math> in the oil than it was in the air
Resolving power will be twice in the oil than it was in the air.
Resolving power will be four times in the oil than it was in the air.
Resolving power will be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>2</mn></mfrac></math> in the oil than it was in the air.
solution:
Resolving power of microscope in air is (R.P)air =2μairsinθ1.22λ=2sinθ1.22λ

(R.P)oil =2μoilsinθ1.22λoil =2sinθ21.22λ

R.Poil=R.Pair×2

Thus, resolving power in oil is twice the resolving power in air.

In accordance with Snell's law, μ=μRμD=sin⁡icsin⁡90o

here ic is the angle of incidence.

μRμD=sin⁡ic

μ=μRμD=sin⁡Asin⁡r

=sinAsin90°-A=sinAcosA

μRμD=tan⁡A.

The critical angle is related to the angle A as tan⁡A=sin⁡θC

A=tan-1⁡sin⁡θC.

subject: Physics
solution:
For a mirror with magnification m = -3 (real image), object and image distances satisfy |u| + |v| = 40 cm.
Since m = v/u = -3, we have v = -3u, giving u + 3u = 40, so u = 10 cm and v = -30 cm.
Using mirror formula: $\frac{1}{f} = \frac{1}{u} + \frac{1}{v} = \frac{1}{10} + \frac{1}{-30} = \frac{3-1}{30} = \frac{1}{15}$
Hence f = -15 cm.

subject: Physics
solution:
Let f0 be the focal length of objective and fe is the focal length of eyepiece.

Given here, f0+fe=30.

Angular magnification of telescope is given by m=f0fe.

So, 2=f0fe⇒f0=2fe

Then, we have f0+f02=30⇒3f02=30

Or, f0=20 cm.
